stages.title: Design
stages.emptyState: Drag ideas here to trigger design phase

stages.title: Validation
stages.emptyState: Landing page live, traffic running, signups tracked here

projects.name: ReceiveLoop
projects.topCompetitors: Stocky (sunsetting), SKUSavvy, Fabrikator, Katana, NetSuite / Brightpearl / other ERPs. Clear wedge: lightweight Shopify-native receiving + open-PO ops instead of full ERP replacement.
projects.pain: Shopify retailers are losing a workable purchase-order receiving workflow as Stocky sunsets, and small teams do not want to buy an ERP just to receive POs, print labels, and reconcile inbound stock.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchants create POs, receive inventory against them, compare vendor invoices, update supplier cost, print barcode labels, and chase open discrepancies. Today that workflow is splintering across Shopify native POs, spreadsheets, manual label printing, and in some cases handwritten vendor paperwork.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: The pain is not theoretical. It recurs on every inbound shipment. Stocky is going away, native Shopify PO receiving changed unexpectedly, key receiving context is missing at scan-time, and stores are being pushed toward overbuilt ERPs or brittle manual workarounds.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: ReceiveLoop
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A Shopify-native receiving workspace for small retailers that replaces the ugly middle ground between disappearing Stocky workflows and expensive ERP suites.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants with physical inventory, especially boutiques and multi-SKU retail teams receiving POs weekly.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: receive PO -> compare invoice -> update landed cost / cost basis -> print barcode labels for exactly received items -> close or partially receive open lines -> reconcile discrepancies.
WHY IT RECURS: Every shipment repeats it; the workflow compounds with more vendors, SKUs, and partial receipts.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) New Purchase Order Process — https://community.shopify.com/t/new-purchase-order-process/629542
2) Stocky App Going Away after August 31 2026 — https://community.shopify.com/t/stocky-app-going-away-after-august-31-2026/587292
3) API endpoint coming soon for Purchase Orders, with Stocky sunset? — https://community.shopify.com/t/api-endpoint-coming-soon-for-purchase-orders-with-stocky-sunset/587136
4) Feature Request: Print Barcode Labels Directly from Purchase Orders (like Stocky) — https://community.shopify.com/t/feature-request-print-barcode-labels-directly-from-purchase-orders-like-stocky/622687
5) Feature Request: Add Cost/Price/Margin Display to Purchase Order Receiving — https://community.shopify.com/t/feature-request-add-cost-price-margin-display-to-purchase-order-receiving/587455
EXACT QUOTES / EXCERPTS:
- "Shopify has completely changed the process to receive a purchase order and complicated it for no apparently reason."
- "I am concerned about hearing that the Stocky App is going away this year... create and receive purchase orders, adjust our costs when receiving... print price labels directly from the PO..."
- "the only resolution at this point is to go pay for an ERP... I am not going back to an external ERP"
- "One critical feature missing... is the ability to print barcode labels directly from a purchase order... Without it, you have to cross-reference the PO manually and print labels separately"
- "This information is critical for my receiving workflow" (re: cost / price / margin visibility while receiving).
CURRENT WORKAROUND: Native Shopify PO + transfer flow, spreadsheets, separate label tools, handwritten vendor paperwork, or moving up to ERP.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: More clicks, context switching, manual cross-referencing, higher receiving error rate, and much higher software cost if forced into ERP.
COMPETITORS: Stocky (sunsetting), SKUSavvy, Fabrikator, Katana, NetSuite, Brightpearl.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: SKUSavvy and ERP-style tools typically push stores into materially higher monthly spend than a single-purpose receiving layer; NetSuite / Brightpearl are quote-based enterprise buys.
WHY NOW: Stocky sunset + Shopify PO changes created a fresh migration window and visible frustration in 2026.
WEDGE / WHY THIS COULD STILL WIN: Do not replace all inventory ops. Own the receiving station: open PO queue, partial receipt workflow, invoice match, delta alerts, cost capture, and print labels for exactly received units.
VALIDATION ANGLE: Concierge MVP for 5-10 Stocky-dependent stores; manually map their PO receipt workflow and prove time saved per shipment.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community threads + outreach to stores publicly discussing Stocky sunset and PO pain.
RISK FLAGS: Shopify may improve native PO features; some stores may still choose full inventory suites.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API, Shopify Flow hooks where available, barcode/label-print integrations, PDF/invoice parsing, vendor CSV import/export. Product shape is an ops overlay, not a full ERP.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: If receiving is weekly or daily, saving even 20-40 minutes per PO and reducing inventory mistakes pays quickly. The emotional ROI is also strong: merchants are explicitly saying they do not want to buy an ERP just to regain basic receiving functionality.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Manual workaround stack: native Shopify PO changes, spreadsheets, separate label apps, and inbox/PDF juggling. Heavy workaround stack: ERP migration. Neither is attractive for SMB retailers that mainly need a reliable receiving cockpit.

projects.name: APRelay
projects.topCompetitors: Sufio, QuickBooks/Xero connectors, Wholesale Gorilla add-ons, custom Flow/webhook builds, ERP A/R modules. Wedge is Shopify B2B receivables ops for merchants who do not want accounting-suite complexity driving customer reminders.
projects.pain: Shopify B2B sellers cannot cleanly manage alternate A/P contacts, partial payments, overdue reminders, and statement-style follow-up inside the operational workflow they already use.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: A small wholesale team takes orders in Shopify, invoices on net terms, then tracks alternate A/P emails, partial bank-transfer payments, overdue balances, and month-end statements in spreadsheets or ad hoc emails outside Shopify.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This is recurring collections work, not a one-time setup problem. Every invoice cycle reintroduces follow-up, partial payment reconciliation, and the mismatch between the buyer contact who ordered and the A/P contact who actually pays.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: APRelay
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A lightweight Shopify B2B receivables copilot that routes invoices to the right A/P contact, tracks partial payments, and automates sane reminder cadences without spreadsheet glue.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify Plus / wholesale merchants offering net terms to repeat B2B buyers.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: create invoice -> send to wrong ordering email -> manually forward to A/P -> log alternate A/P address in spreadsheet -> receive partial payment by bank transfer -> manually calculate remaining balance -> send month-end overdue statement and reminders.
WHY IT RECURS: Every invoice cycle, every late payer, every customer with a separate purchasing contact and payables contact.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) How can I manage partial payments for wholesale customers on Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-can-i-manage-partial-payments-for-wholesale-customers-on-shopify/235601
2) How to send automatically invoices and payment reminders to alternate A/P email through Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-to-send-automatically-invoices-and-payment-reminders-to-alternate-a-p-email-through-shopify/327432
3) Tag orders that are 30 days overdue with Flow — https://community.shopify.com/t/tag-orders-that-are-30-days-overdue-with-flow/295506
4) Shopify Flow: daily “until paid” reminders + 5-days-before due — https://community.shopify.com/t/shopify-flow-daily-until-paid-reminders-5-days-before-due-loops-scheduled-time-order-context/572816
EXACT QUOTES / EXCERPTS:
- "They require that I send them statements at month end with the amounts overdue and reflecting the amounts paid."
- "I don’t understand why Shopify does not have a very basic feature where I can input the amount that was paid on an invoice and it reflects the outstanding amount"
- "We have to track the alternate email addresses with a spreadsheet outside of Shopify and manually send invoices and payment reminders."
- "Can someone help me configure Flow to tag orders that are past due by 30 days?"
- "It works, but it’s messy... I had to stack a long chain of steps: wait 1 day -> check if paid -> if not, send reminder -> repeat."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: spreadsheets, manual email forwarding, custom Flow chains, outside accounting tools, or custom apps.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: fragmented customer history, operator error, wrong-recipient reminders, weak visibility into outstanding balances, and brittle automation.
COMPETITORS: Sufio, QuickBooks/Xero connectors, ERP A/R modules, bespoke webhooks.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: Sufio and adjacent invoice tools typically start as subscription apps, while ERP/accounting-suite alternatives quickly become much broader and heavier than the narrow collections workflow merchants are describing.
WHY NOW: Shopify B2B usage is rising, but operational receivables inside Shopify are still visibly underpowered.
WEDGE / WHY THIS COULD STILL WIN: Focus narrowly on operational collections inside Shopify: alternate A/P routing, partial payment ledger, reminder cadence, and statement sending. Do not try to replace the accounting system.
VALIDATION ANGLE: Pilot with 5 wholesale stores using net terms; measure DSO improvement and hours saved at month-end.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community B2B / wholesale threads and direct outreach to merchants using Wholesale Gorilla or net terms.
RISK FLAGS: Some merchants may prefer to push everything into QuickBooks/Xero; payment-term volume may be concentrated in larger stores.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API, webhooks, Shopify Flow, email sending, optional QuickBooks/Xero sync, customer-company metafields for A/P contacts, internal ledger for partial-payment state.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: Collections friction hits cash flow directly. A merchant with even a modest wholesale book can justify a focused tool if it reduces overdue balances, manual follow-up time, and misrouted invoices.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Today the options are spreadsheet glue, brittle Flow chains, or migrating the whole problem into accounting/ERP systems. That is too much overhead for merchants who simply want their invoices to go to the right person and outstanding balances to stay accurate.

projects.name: ReturnRadar
projects.topCompetitors: Loop Returns, AfterShip Returns, ParcelPanel/ParcelWILL, Frate, Rich Returns. Wedge is internal ops control: SLA queue, exception tracking, and manual-workflow cleanup for brands not ready to re-platform returns.
projects.pain: Growing Shopify brands often still run returns through email + spreadsheet + manual refund/restock work, and things fall through the cracks long before they are willing to buy a full customer-facing returns suite.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Customers email return requests, staff looks up orders, creates labels one by one, tracks return state in a spreadsheet, manually restocks inventory, and issues refunds manually. Teams then chase open returns that are stuck in progress or broken by 3PL limitations.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: The problem compounds with volume. As order count rises, the internal exception queue grows faster than the team can reliably manage, causing slow response times, wrong-item refunds, missed restocks, and ugly customer support follow-up.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: ReturnRadar
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: An internal return-ops dashboard for Shopify brands that are not ready for a full returns platform but urgently need SLA tracking, exception queues, and spreadsheet-free follow-up.
TARGET BUYER: Apparel and consumer brands on Shopify with growing return volume and a still-manual back office.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: inbox return request -> manual order lookup -> create label -> spreadsheet log -> wait for package -> manually restock -> manually refund -> chase stuck returns / 3PL mismatches.
WHY IT RECURS: Returns are operationally repetitive and spike with order volume, seasons, sizing issues, and 3PL handoffs.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Return management workflow - looking for advice — https://community.shopify.com/t/return-management-workflow-looking-for-advice/586883
2) Creating a flow to email me when a return has been open for two weeks — https://community.shopify.com/t/creating-a-flow-to-email-me-when-a-return-has-been-open-for-two-weeks/272361
3) How are you handling exchanges when your 3PL doesn’t support Shopify Exchange API? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-are-you-handling-exchanges-when-your-3pl-doesn-t-support-shopify-exchange-api/590580
4) I need the best and most affordable return and exchange app for our store. — https://community.shopify.com/t/i-need-the-best-and-most-affordable-return-and-exchange-app-for-our-store/374315
EXACT QUOTES / EXCERPTS:
- "Our return volume has grown significantly ... and our manual process is becoming unsustainable."
- "Creates return labels one by one" / "Tracks returns in spreadsheet" / "Taking 10-15 hours per week" / "Prone to errors"
- "I want to automate it to make sure nothing falls through the cracks."
- "Our 3PL does not support Shopify’s Exchange API."
- "I’m looking for the best and most affordable return and exchange app for our store."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: internal email inbox, spreadsheets, DIY Flow alerts, and piecemeal customer-facing return apps layered over manual ops.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: returns get stuck, support gets slower, refunds or restocks are missed, and operators still need a manual exception list even after buying front-end tooling.
COMPETITORS: Loop Returns, AfterShip Returns, ParcelPanel/ParcelWILL, Frate, Rich Returns.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: ParcelWILL/ParcelPanel was cited in-thread at free for up to 6 returns per month and $29/month for 50 returns; bigger suites like Loop / AfterShip typically move upmarket quickly as volume grows.
WHY NOW: More brands have grown into meaningful return volume, but many still are not ready for a full migration to enterprise-grade returns software.
WEDGE / WHY THIS COULD STILL WIN: Sell the internal ops layer, not the whole customer portal: SLA aging, stuck-return alerts, refund/restock checklist, 3PL exception notes, and per-return accountability.
VALIDATION ANGLE: Start with a Gmail/Shopify hybrid MVP that turns return emails plus order state into an aging queue; prove reduction in hours per week and unresolved returns.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community threads about manual returns, plus outreach to apparel stores with visible support-driven return workflows.
RISK FLAGS: Competitive category; wedge must stay sharp around internal operations and exception handling.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API, Shopify Flow, email parsing, shipping-label integrations, optional 3PL webhooks, and lightweight internal tasking rather than a full branded portal.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: A store burning 10-15 hours per week on returns already told us the ROI story. If the tool cuts that in half and reduces refund/restock errors, it pays for itself fast even before improving CX.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The low end is spreadsheet chaos. The high end is a full returns platform that can still leave internal exception handling unresolved. The wedge is an internal control tower for teams sitting between those extremes.
